creator: mat@mat.cc
text:
Ok.

So, ok, let me begin that we don't support building with a non-default
compiler.

So, I tried with :
make stage USE_GCC=4.8
and it worked all right, like I figure it would.
Then I tried :
make stage CC=gcc48 CXX=g++48 CPP=cpp48
And it failed, a bit like I expected it to fail. When you do that, you're
missing a few other bits that should be there, like :
CFLAGS=        -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48
CXXFLAGS=      -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48
LDFLAGS=       -Wl,-rpath=/usr/local/lib/gcc48 -L/usr/local/lib/gcc48

If you want ports to build with gcc 4.8 when it is said they need gcc, you
want to put these in your make.conf :
FAVORITE_COMPILER=gcc
GCC_DEFAULT_VERSION=4.8

But it may fail in some other ways, it's not supported, though, patch are
welcome :-)

-- 
Mathieu Arnold
